summary:
Calcium in milk binds fluorides through chemical precipitation, reducing their systemic absorption; this principle forms strong scientific support regardless of limited comparative trial data. Immediate consultation with poison control is also recommended but does not supersede the benefit of administering an appropriate binder such as milk when available. 

Final Answer: $\boxed{\text{C}}$

Only summarize the agent’s reasoning—do NOT include your own interpretation or add new content beyond what appears directly in the text above.

Calcium in milk binds fluoride through chemical precipitation, reducing its systemic absorption based on well-established physicochemical principles. Despite limitations in specific comparative study data, these mechanisms provide sufficient rationale to recommend milk as the preferred first-line measure after exposure.  

$\boxed{\text{C}}$
